variables.globalPolicyXml:
<!--
  Global APIM Policy
  Applied to all APIs. Handles CORS, request metrics, and tracing.
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <!-- CORS for local development (VS Code / MCP clients) -->
        <cors allow-credentials="true">
            <allowed-origins>
                <origin>http://localhost:3000</origin>
                <origin>https://portal.azure.com</origin>
                <origin>https://vscode.dev</origin>
            </allowed-origins>
            <allowed-methods preflight-result-max-age="300">
                <method>GET</method>
                <method>POST</method>
                <method>DELETE</method>
                <method>OPTIONS</method>
            </allowed-methods>
            <allowed-headers>
                <header>Content-Type</header>
                <header>Authorization</header>
                <header>Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key</header>
                <header>Mcp-Session-Id</header>
                <header>Accept</header>
            </allowed-headers>
            <expose-headers>
                <header>Mcp-Session-Id</header>
                <header>Content-Type</header>
            </expose-headers>
        </cors>
        <!-- Emit custom metric for request counting -->
        <emit-metric name="mcp-gateway-requests" value="1" namespace="apim-mcp-gateway">
            <dimension name="API ID" value="@(context.Api.Id)" />
            <dimension name="Subscription ID" value="@(context.Subscription.Id)" />
            <dimension name="Client IP" value="@(context.Request.IpAddress)" />
            <dimension name="Operation" value="@(context.Operation.Id)" />
        </emit-metric>
        <!-- Tracing for diagnostics -->
        <trace source="global-policy" severity="information">
            <message>@($"Request: {context.Request.Method} {context.Request.Url.Path} | API: {context.Api.Name} | Subscription: {context.Subscription.Name}")</message>
        </trace>
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <forward-request />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
        <trace source="global-policy" severity="error">
            <message>@($"Error: {context.LastError.Message} | Reason: {context.LastError.Reason} | API: {context.Api.Name}")</message>
        </trace>
    </on-error>
</policies>
variables.mcpPolicyXml:
<!--
  MCP Passthrough Policy
  Applied to MCP server APIs. Handles rate limiting, optional Entra ID
  validation, MCP primitives filtering, and MCP protocol transparency
  (Streamable HTTP / JSON-RPC 2.0).
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />
        <!--
          Optional: Entra ID (Azure AD) token validation.
          Uncomment the block below when OAuth 2.1 authentication is required.
          Configure named values: entra-tenant-id, entra-client-app-ids.
        -->
        <!--
        <validate-azure-ad-token tenant-id="{{entra-tenant-id}}" output-token-variable-name="jwt-token">
            <client-application-ids>
                <application-id>{{entra-client-app-ids}}</application-id>
            </client-application-ids>
        </validate-azure-ad-token>
        -->
        <!-- Rate limit per MCP session to prevent abuse -->
        <rate-limit-by-key
            calls="60"
            renewal-period="60"
            counter-key="@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("Mcp-Session-Id", context.Request.IpAddress))"
            increment-condition="@(context.Response.StatusCode >= 200 && context.Response.StatusCode < 400)" />
        <!-- Trace MCP-specific request details -->
        <trace source="mcp-passthrough" severity="information">
            <message>@{
                var sessionId = context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("Mcp-Session-Id", "no-session");
                return $"MCP Request | Session: {sessionId} | Method: {context.Request.Method} | Path: {context.Request.Url.Path}";
            }</message>
        </trace>
        <!--
          MCP Primitives Filtering (tools, prompts, resources).
          Reads per-server filter config from Named Value: mcp-primitives-<serverName>.
          If no Named Value exists, all primitives are allowed (passthrough).
        -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Request.Method == "POST")">
                <set-variable name="mcp-request-body" value="@(context.Request.Body.As<string>(preserveContent: true))" />
                <set-variable name="mcp-primitives-config-raw" value="{{mcp-primitives-config}}" />
                <set-variable name="mcp-jsonrpc-method" value="@{
                    var body = (string)context.Variables["mcp-request-body"];
                    if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(body)) { return ""; }
                    try {
                        var json = Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject.Parse(body);
                        return json["method"]?.ToString() ?? "";
                    } catch { return ""; }
                }" />
                <choose>
                    <when condition="@{
                        var m = (string)context.Variables["mcp-jsonrpc-method"];
                        return m == "tools/call" || m == "prompts/get" || m == "resources/read";
                    }">
                        <set-variable name="mcp-primitive-name" value="@{
                            var body = (string)context.Variables["mcp-request-body"];
                            var method = (string)context.Variables["mcp-jsonrpc-method"];
                            try {
                                var json = Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject.Parse(body);
                                var p = json["params"];
                                if (p == null) { return ""; }
                                return method == "resources/read"
                                    ? (p["uri"]?.ToString() ?? "")
                                    : (p["name"]?.ToString() ?? "");
                            } catch { return ""; }
                        }" />
                        <set-variable name="mcp-primitive-type" value="@{
                            var m = (string)context.Variables["mcp-jsonrpc-method"];
                            if (m == "tools/call") { return "tools"; }
                            if (m == "prompts/get") { return "prompts"; }
                            return "resources";
                        }" />
                        <set-variable name="mcp-filter-blocked" value="@{
                            var primitiveType = (string)context.Variables["mcp-primitive-type"];
                            var name = (string)context.Variables["mcp-primitive-name"];
                            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) { return "false"; }

                            var allConfigsJson = (string)context.Variables["mcp-primitives-config-raw"];
                            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(allConfigsJson) || allConfigsJson.StartsWith("{{")) { return "false"; }

                            try {
                                var serverName = context.Api.Id.Replace("-api", "");
                                var allConfigs = Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject.Parse(allConfigsJson);
                                var serverConfig = allConfigs[serverName];
                                if (serverConfig == null) { return "false"; }
                                var config = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)serverConfig;
                                var filter = config[primitiveType];
                                if (filter == null) { return "false"; }
                                var policy = filter["policy"]?.ToString() ?? "allowAll";

                                if (policy == "denyAll") { return "true"; }
                                if (policy == "allowAll") { return "false"; }

                                if (policy == "denyList") {
                                    var denied = filter["denied"]?.ToObject<string[]>() ?? new string[0];
                                    foreach (var d in denied) {
                                        if (d.EndsWith("*") ? name.StartsWith(d.TrimEnd('*')) : name == d) {
                                            return "true";
                                        }
                                    }
                                    return "false";
                                }
                                if (policy == "allowList") {
                                    var allowed = filter["allowed"]?.ToObject<string[]>() ?? new string[0];
                                    foreach (var a in allowed) {
                                        if (a.EndsWith("*") ? name.StartsWith(a.TrimEnd('*')) : name == a) {
                                            return "false";
                                        }
                                    }
                                    return "true";
                                }
                                return "false";
                            } catch { return "false"; }
                        }" />
                        <choose>
                            <when condition="@((string)context.Variables["mcp-filter-blocked"] == "true")">
                                <return-response>
                                    <set-status code="403" reason="Forbidden" />
                                    <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                                        <value>application/json</value>
                                    </set-header>
                                    <set-body>@{
                                        var body = (string)context.Variables["mcp-request-body"];
                                        var id = "null";
                                        try { id = Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject.Parse(body)["id"]?.ToString() ?? "null"; } catch {}
                                        var pType = (string)context.Variables["mcp-primitive-type"];
                                        var pName = (string)context.Variables["mcp-primitive-name"];
                                        return $"{{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":{id},\"error\":{{\"code\":-32600,\"message\":\"Blocked by governance policy: {pType}/{pName} is not allowed on this gateway.\"}}}}";
                                    }</set-body>
                                </return-response>
                            </when>
                        </choose>
                    </when>
                </choose>
            </when>
        </choose>
        <!-- Set backend to the configured MCP server -->
        <set-backend-service backend-id="@(context.Api.Id.Replace("-api", "-backend"))" />
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <base />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
        <base />
        <!-- Ensure MCP Session ID is forwarded in response -->
        <set-header name="Mcp-Session-Id" exists-action="skip">
            <value>@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("Mcp-Session-Id", ""))</value>
        </set-header>
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
        <base />
    </on-error>
</policies>
variables.aoaiPolicyXml:
<!--
  Azure OpenAI Policy
  Applied to Azure OpenAI APIs exposed through the AI Gateway.
  Provides token-based rate limiting, token usage metrics, and
  managed identity authentication to the backend.
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />
        <!--
          Optional: Entra ID (Azure AD) token validation for client-side auth.
          Uncomment when OAuth 2.1 authentication is required for callers.
        -->
        <!--
        <validate-azure-ad-token tenant-id="{{entra-tenant-id}}" output-token-variable-name="jwt-token">
            <client-application-ids>
                <application-id>{{entra-client-app-ids}}</application-id>
            </client-application-ids>
        </validate-azure-ad-token>
        -->
        <!-- Token-based rate limiting (tokens per minute) -->
        <llm-token-limit
            tokens-per-minute="10000"
            counter-key="@(context.Subscription.Id)"
            estimate-prompt-tokens="true"
            tokens-consumed-header-name="x-tokens-consumed"
            remaining-tokens-header-name="x-tokens-remaining" />
        <!-- Authenticate to Azure OpenAI backend using managed identity -->
        <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com" />
        <!-- Trace Azure OpenAI request details -->
        <trace source="aoai-policy" severity="information">
            <message>@($"Azure OpenAI Request | Subscription: {context.Subscription.Name} | Deployment: {context.Request.MatchedParameters["deployment-id"]}")</message>
        </trace>
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <base />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
        <base />
        <!-- Emit token usage metrics from the response -->
        <emit-metric name="aoai-token-usage" value="1" namespace="apim-mcp-gateway">
            <dimension name="API ID" value="@(context.Api.Id)" />
            <dimension name="Subscription ID" value="@(context.Subscription.Id)" />
            <dimension name="Client IP" value="@(context.Request.IpAddress)" />
            <dimension name="Model" value="@(context.Request.MatchedParameters.GetValueOrDefault("deployment-id", "unknown"))" />
        </emit-metric>
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
        <base />
    </on-error>
</policies>
